observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to trace and analyze the long outstanding reconciling items. Copies of bank debit and credit memoranda be secured at the earliest possible time and reconciliation with the depository bank be made periodically and immediately after receipt of the statements of account to timely adjust or correct the balances of the agency accounts; and
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to review periodically the released checks and inform the Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately;
Submit the Schedule of Dormant Accounts quarterly on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month; and,
Exert all efforts to expedite liquidation of cash advances of officers and employees who have already died or are no longer in service. Consider the preparation of a request for write-off of the Receivable accounts which were considered dormant pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008.
Furthermore, we recommended that management refrain from granting additional cash advance if the previous cash advances were not fully settled or accounted for. Consider possible withholding of salaries of those officials and employees who persistently refuse to cooperate and still fails to liquidate their cash advances within the timeframe as required by the present regulation.
We would like to emphasize that the Circular shall not be interpreted to condone the accounts written-off nor to extinguish the obligations. The Management shall continue to exert effort to collect the accounts appearing in the Registry of Accounts Written-Off (Annex 10) or Registry of Derecognized Dormant Accounts (Annex 11) when circumstances warrant. Further, this shall not be construed as a ground to exonerate from liability the officers/employees for infidelity in the custody of documents.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to identify the projects covering the balances of Advances to Contractors and reconcile the balances. Also, to exert more efforts to fast track and exhaust all administrative and legal means for the immediate recovery of long outstanding advances to contractors. Furthermore, require the contractors concerned to refund the unrecouped amount for terminated and completed projects or deduct the amount from progress billings of other existing contracts of the said contractors.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct its General Services Officer to conduct semestral inventory of supplies and prepare reports and submit the results of the inventory taking to the Municipal Accountant for the latter to prepare the necessary adjusting entries where appropriate, for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
Furthermore, instruct the GSO to maintain Stock Card for each type of supplies to record all receipts and issuances and the Municipal Accountant to prepare its Subsidiary Ledger Cards. Likewise, regularly update the stock card of each item for the issuances made based on a summary of issuances made on a daily basis, and each time items are received to properly monitor against under/over stocking of supplies, and to establish control against theft or abuse of the inventory items.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that Management instruct the Municipal Assessor to exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title in the name of the Municipal Government of Tambulig pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445 and Section 449, Volume 1 of GAAM. Ensure that a budget be allocated for this purpose. Further, that the Municipal Accountant provide this Office the basis for recording the Land account with the total amount of ₱1.3 million to ascertain whether valuation of these properties was proper and reasonable.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management exert efforts to locate pertinent documents that could provide details and identification of the projects undertaken under the CIP accounts and to henceforth maintain subsidiary ledgers on a per project basis. Once identification of the projects had been established and that construction of the same has already been completed, CIP accounts be properly closed to appropriate asset accounts. Furthermore, the Municipal Engineer to submit the required Certificate of Completion of the completed projects to the Municipal Accountant for the adjustment/closing of the Construction in Progress accounts to its appropriate asset accounts.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations as follows:
The Inventory Committee conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipality and render a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System;
The General Services Officer:
Render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map;
Regularly maintain and update the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
The Municipal Accountant:
Prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each of the depreciable component at the end of the year.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that Management:
direct the Municipal General Services Officer to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with the GSIS and submit the PIF to the GSIS; and,
direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide appropriation for the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to periodically review the books account so as to monitor and adjust any inaccurate and unrecorded transactions to avoid negative balances. Further, the Municipal Accountant make adjusting entries on the identified errors and double recording of transactions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the Human Resource Officer to always follow up with the GSIS through email and/or in person and request for documents and for the condonation of interest, if any;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with Municipal Treasurer in identifying the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipality to settle the obligations; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare schedule or subsidiary ledger and record the amounts in the proper account to have accurate and reliable Financial Statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in identifying the employees with unremitted contributions and reconcile with the balance as of 31 December 2023 in order to settle the obligations of the Municipality. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Philhealth within the prescribed period.

Recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant trace the transactions recorded in the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments. Likewise, provide the Agency Auditors the JEV evidencing the recording of the adjusting entries; and
The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to make the necessary fund transfers to settle these accounts and allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the BAC Chairperson, submit a justification with certified documentation as to the reason why the procurement of the project and the award thereof pushed through despite the absence of an existing appropriation.
We likewise recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan, through the Municipal Vice Mayor, submit a justification with certified documentation as to the reason why an approved appropriation ordinance on the project did not push through despite the existence of an approved loan with the DBP.

We recommended that management to require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to trace and reconcile the balance of Cash in Bank accounts. Copies of bank debit and credit memoranda should be secured at the earliest possible time and reconciliation with the depository bank should be made periodically and immediately after receipt of the statements of account to timely adjust or correct the balances of the agency accounts. Documents from the bank should be persistently secured to serve as basis for the adjustment or corrections and recording of charges made by the bank on the Municipality’s accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the technical assistance of the Municipal Engineer:

Issue an Executive Order creating the CPES – IU, identifying its members and responsibilities or integrating the CPES function in an existing unit with similar functions which shall act as the CPES – IU in accordance with CPES Implementing Guidelines for Infrastructure Projects; 

Formulate and adopt specific Implementing Guidelines for the evaluation of project types which do not have specific CPES Guidelines; and,

Ensure the proper training of personnel involved in CPES implementation.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Treasurer comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with the GSIS; and,

The Municipal Budget Officer provide appropriation for the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point personnel establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, to come up with gender-responsive and gender-related program, projects and activities and incorporate these in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in identifying the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipality to settle the obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the GSIS within the prescribed period; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare schedule or subsidiary ledger and record the amounts in the proper account to have accurate and reliable Financial Statements.

OIC Market Supervisor immediately demand for the immediate payment of the delinquent accounts. Should the stall owners fail to pay, institute other remedies in addition to the immediate cancellation of the lease contracts; and

The Municipal Accountant make necessary adjusting entry to record the receivable in the books of accounts and provide the Auditors of copies of the journal entry voucher.
The OIC - Market Supervisor ensure that contracts of lease be executed between the Municipality and the qualified stallholders before they would be allowed to continue their business operations.
The MSWDO, being the GAD Focal Person, conduct the HGDG test to better identify the gender issues and take a better approach in resolving them.

We recommended that management submit the necessary reports as mentioned above and its corresponding supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and approved before they will be paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts.

Also, institute as a matter of policy, that the quantity of fuel issued to every vehicle must correspond only to the vehicle’s average consumption per distance of travel plus 10% allowance.

Municipal Accountant maintain a separate special account in the General Fund for loans wherein the receipts and expenditures thereof are recorded so that accurate and separate information on the status of loans were readily available for decision making purposes and observed timely payments of its loan amortization and interests due to avoid incurrence of penalties on the failure of such.

Require the Accountable Officers concerned to refund the unremitted collections under their custody without prejudice to the filing of malversation case under Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code.  Management should impose appropriate sanctions to ensure that subject funds shall be collected by the LGU like deducting from the monthly salary or any other claim of the Accountable Officers.  Subject amount of money is very much needed for the operations of the LGU.

Further, require that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer should implement a strong internal control in the monitoring of all collections, as this is one of the major functions of her office, to ensure that income or collections of the Municipality would be correctly receipted and deposited intact; correctly recorded, reconciled in the books and properly reported in the period in which they were earned; and those accountable forms are duly accounted for.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal GSO to complete of the conduct of the annual physical count of PPEs and Inventories and to complete and reconcile the prepared RPCPPE and RPCI and accordingly prepare the PIF to be submitted to the Audit Team and GSIS.

10.8 We reiterated our recommendations for Management:
To direct the Municipal General Services Officer to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with the GSIS; and,
To direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide appropriation for the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Determine the legitimacy of the transactions, and if found to be valid claims, communicate with the concerned payees for the payment/settlement of the accounts;
Revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund those that proved to be with no valid claims; and
Conduct periodic review and monitoring of the Accounts Payable account so that this account will consist only of valid claims against the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the Human Resource Officer to follow up with GSIS not just through email but in person and request for documents and for the condonation of any interest;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with Municipal Treasurer in identifying the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipality to settle the obligations; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare schedule or subsidiary ledger and record the amounts in the proper account to have accurate and reliable Financial Statements.

We recommended the following:
13.12.1 The Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant the actual receivables due at the beginning of the year based on the assessment roll from the Municipal Assessor’s Office;
13.12.2 The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor regularly reconcile the assessments and taxes due in order that the receivables due at the beginning of the year truly reflect the receivables due for the LGU at the start of the year; and
13.12.3the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile the balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income with the Schedule of Delinquent Taxpayers prepared by the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the affected accounts in the Statement of Financial Position of the Municipality are fairly presented.

We recommended that General Services Officer submit the necessary reports on time as mentioned above and its corresponding supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and approved before they will be paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts.
Also, institute as a matter of policy, that the quantity of fuel issued to every vehicle must correspond only to the vehicle’s average consumption per distance of travel plus 10% allowance.

We recommended the following:
the Municipal Accountant submit the schedules to support the Prior Years’ Adjustments account; and
the Municipal Accountant ensure that the necessary disclosures be adequately made in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

17.4 We recommended that the GAD Focal Point personnel accomplish the gender analysis with updated GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics, to come up with gender-responsive and gender-related program, projects and activities and incorporate these in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

18.3 We recommended that Management adopt policies and internal control measures to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that the monthly net take home pay of any employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 49 of RA 11518. Among the policies to be included is for the Municipal Accountant to not approve any loan application if the same falls beyond the prescribed minimum take home pay.

19.7 We recommended that Management utilize and fully operationalize its recruitment process through its Human Resource Management Division (HRMD) to ensure that personnel who possess the qualification standards of funded plantilla positions are filled up to achieve the purpose/s for which funds are appropriated. The Municipal HRMO should uphold the priority needs, desired service requirement and financial capacity of the Local Government consistent with the principles of efficiency, economy and effectiveness.

We recommended the following:
MEEDO Manager demand for the immediate payment of the delinquent accounts. Otherwise, immediately cancel the lease contracts with the stall owners;
MEEDO Manager demand immediate payment of delinquent water rentals; and
MEEDO Manager provide the Municipal Accountant with copies of delinquent market space & stall rentals and water rentals for the latter to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the receivable and income from these sources.
21.5 We recommended that the Local School Board maximize the utilization of the SEF through the implementation of the programs, activities and projects included in the SEF Budget.

22.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents of the subject audited transactions. Moving forward, the Municipal Accountant be more thorough in ensuring that the supporting documents are attached prior to the submission of the same.

23.8 We recommended the following corrective measures for the aforesaid deficiencies:
Strict compliance on the documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001; and
Strictly comply with Section 22 of the Civil Service Memorandum Circular No. 41, series of 1998 and the Civil Service Resolution No. 000034.

24.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate special account in the General Fund for loans wherein the receipts and expenditures thereof are recorded so that accurate and separate information on the status of loans were readily available for decision making purposes and observe timely payments of its loan amortization and interests due to avoid incurrence of penalties on the failure of such.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
26.8.1 Create a special committee, headed by the Municipal Accountant, to craft the internal rules on the use of digital signature or other types of electronic signature on electronic documents. The internal rules shall include provisions with regards to the sanctions for unauthorized and illegal use of digital certificates or electronic signatures; and
26.8.2 Submit the Management Representation or Policy Statement on the use of signature on electronic documents in their operations to their respective Auditors, together with the approved internal rules.

Reason for non-implementation:
The Municipality has still not completed the Physical Count of Properties and COA Circular No. 2020-006 can be used to clean the PPE accounts of the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to record procurements involving inventories following the asset recognition principle where an asset is recognized when it is probable that future economic benefit will flow to the agency and it can be measured reliably.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Transfer the noted payables to their proper payable accounts through an adjusting entry;
For the unidentified payees, reconcile the same through available records. Obligation records in the Office of the Municipal Budget Officer may also be resorted to; and
Establish subsidiary ledgers on the identifiable payees of the account.

We recommended that:

As the reasons of the discrepancies are already identified, the Accounting Office take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts of the respective funds; and

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to issue the necessary ORs in order for the balances to be dropped from the books of accounts

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant:
Identify the reason of the discrepancy and remit the amount due to the BIR; and 

The necessity to maintain a schedule of the accounts and prepare the subsidiary ledger and record the amounts in the proper account to have accurate and reliable Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the details of the Loans Payable-Domestic account in the NFS, to provide clarity and reflect information that are pertinent to decision-making, in keeping with IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revise the collection and remittance policies relative to the sub-collectors wherein the remittances of the sub-collectors concerned will pass through their respective AOs in order that the latter would be informed of the transactions entered into by the former.

We recommended the following:
The Chief Executive immediately stop the practice and not designate to any single individual the collection or liquidation and disbursing functions. Instead, these have to be performed by different personnel; and

The Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Treasurer make a thorough review of its plantilla and the functions performed by the LGU personnel in order to ensure that no two incompatible responsibilities are performed by one employee.
We recommended that the Local School Board maximize the utilization of the SEF through the implementation of the programs, activities and projects included in the SEF Budget.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents noted. Prospectively, the Municipal Accountant examine the completeness of the supporting documents of every transaction before payment shall be done consistent with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and the applicable procurement laws.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act without complying with the requirements of Item 3.5 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and the required OSS form. We also enjoin BAC to be continually updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.
We recommended that:
The BAC, the BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant submit justification as to why there has been noted difference is the signatures of the winning bidder; and 

The Municipal Accountant scrutinize all of the supporting documents to ensure consistency of signatures appearing thereto.

We recommended that management promptly implement PPAs to ensure that socio-economic benefits expected to be provided by these projects will be enjoyed by constituents within the budget year and ensure continuous development in the community.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant reconcile the balance of the Trust Liability-DRRM with the Cash in Bank 5% DRRM Fund and establish a sufficient cash back-up for the said trust account; and

The Municipal Accountant make disclosure of the unreconciled amount in the Notes to Financial Statement of the Trust Liabilities – DRRM, Cash in Bank 5% DRRM funds and total unutilized balance of the DRRM funds

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in identifying the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipality to settle the obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-Ibig within the prescribed period; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare schedule or subsidiary ledger and record the amounts in the proper account to have accurate and reliable Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in identifying the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipality to settle the obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Philhealth within the prescribed period; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare schedule or subsidiary ledger and record the amounts in the proper account to have accurate and reliable Financial Statements.

the Municipal Budget Officer stop the charging of allowances and other benefits to the heads of the NGAs as Representation Expenses. Instead, the same shall be appropriated to OMOE as required in DBM Budget Execution Guidelines No. 2004-1; and

the Municipal Budget Officer closely coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the proper account to be used following the Revised Chart of Accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015.

The Local Chief Executive, through the GAD Focal Person:

include in the GAD Plan and Budget the PPAs that would be implemented during the year; and

ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD PPAs and the judicious utilization of the GAD Budget.

The MPDO together with all other officials and employees concerned to facilitate adequate planning and budgeting for development projects. Identify the individual PPAs in the AIP and refrain from inputting lump sum/ generic term appropriation.

Management and the Municipality Solid Waste Management Board provide personal protective equipment to garbage collectors, drivers and others involved in the solid waste management as a prevention tool from exposure to a variety of hazards for health and safety. These include but not limited to gloves, foot and eye protection, high visibility clothing, face masks, hard hats and other related safety precaution paraphernalia. The wearing of an identification card is also necessary. 

Annual vaccination and/or necessary food supplements be provided in coordination with the Municipality Health Office.  The Municipality may also require an up-to-date health certificate issued by the Municipal Health Office.

Management complies with the requirements on the submission of the perfected contracts to the Auditor within five working days pursuant to the afore-cited regulations.

Likewise, submit the documents enumerated in Items 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

Management, headed by the Municipal Mayor, plan better the projects that would be charged to specific funds in order to reduce the chances of projects identified to the wrong fund resulting to its non-implementation; and

Ensure that the projects identified should be implemented during the year so that the socio-economic development and environmental targets would be achieved.

Fast track the completion of the MRFs in Barangay Tuluan in order that the temporary dumpsite at Barangay Limamaan could be closed or could be made more compliant with the RA 9003; and

Prioritize the appropriation and implementation of the programs and projects as envisioned in the long-term Solid Waste Management Plan of the municipality.

Management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to initiate the regular/timely release of the quarterly RPT shares for all the barangays of the Municipality pursuant to Section 271 (d) of Republic Act No. 7160.

The accountable officers/collectors shall liquidate their collections at the close of each day by preparing/ accomplishing the RCDs and turnover the collections to the Liquidating Officer. Then, the Liquidating Officer shall perform the procedures for the receipt and verification of collections turned over to her and accomplish the RCDs to summarize the collections and shall turn over intact the collections to the Municipal Treasurer. 

Ensure strict adherence to rules and regulations, appropriate disciplinary action shall be taken against accountable officers who fail to perform their functions and to comply with existing rules and regulations.

Management maximizes the utilization of the 70% Mitigation Fund so as not to defeat the purpose for which the law was created, that is, to address the root causes of vulnerabilities to disasters, strengthening the country’s institutional capacity for disaster risk reduction and management, and building resilience of local communities to disasters including climate change impacts.

Close coordination among LGU officials and other DRRM offices should be done to ensure effective formulation, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of the LDRRM Plan and the LDRRMFIP, as well as the proper utilization and distribution of the LDRRMF.

The Local School Board to:

follow strictly Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG in the budget preparation of the SEF;
submit Quarterly the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF Utilization as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.

The Municipal Budget Officer to approve Obligation Requests (ObRs) and the Local Chief Executive to approve the Disbursement Vouchers for SEF in accordance with the Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balance daily with her cash on hand, and strictly abide with the guidelines in the handling and disposition cash which come into her possession as an accountable officer.

We recommended that the accountable officer faithfully adhere to the existing rules and regulations on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of all cash advances handled by her to facilitate monitoring and verification thereof, prevent loss and/or misuse of government funds and avoid possible shortage in audit, which have penal sanctions. Also, management install sound internal control to deter possible occurrence of misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the accountable officers’ fidelity bond be increased in accordance to the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 2-99 dated August 6, 2009 since their total actual cash collections/ advances were beyond their bonded maximum accountability as shown in the above computation.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer initiates in proposing to the Municipal Mayor the OIC-Municipal Treasurer with appropriate position commensurate to her assigned responsibilities. Should she lacks the necessary qualification, the Municipal Mayor should appoint a qualified personnel in the position of Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that:
the guidelines in the handling, custody and disposition of the cashbook as stipulated in Sec 174, GAAM Vol. I be strictly adhered to; and
take up into account the balance of accountability duly received from the previous municipal treasurer.
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer update his cashbooks and record immediately all transactions for the day in consonance with COA Circular No. 2002-003.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to adhere strictly with the provisions of Section 26, Volume I, COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002 for LGUs.

We recommended that the accountable officers/collectors shall liquidate their collections at the close of each day by preparing/accomplishing the RCDs and turnover the collections to the Liquidating Officer. Then, the Liquidating Officer shall perform the procedures for the receipt and verification of collections turned over to her and accomplish the RCDs to summarize the collections and shall turn over intact the collections to the Municipal Treasurer.
To ensure strict adherence to rules and regulations, appropriate disciplinary action shall be taken against accountable officers who fail to perform their functions and to comply with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the 70% Mitigation Fund so as not to defeat the purpose for which the law was created, that is, to address the root causes of vulnerabilities to disasters, strengthening the country’s institutional capacity for disaster risk reduction and management, and building resilience of local communities to disasters including climate change impacts.
Close coordination among LGU officials and other DRRM offices should be done to ensure effective formulation, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of the LDRRM Plan and the LDRRMFIP, as well as the proper utilization and distribution of the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that policies and guidelines as provided in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, s2012 dated November 29, 2012, pursuant to Republic Act No 7305 entitled as “Magna Carta of Public Health Workers” should be strictly followed in view of its efficient and effective implementation to ensure the legality, validity and propriety of subject transactions.

Require the refund of the excess subsistence allowance claimed by the health personnel and refrain from claiming such in the future transactions to avoid possible disallowance in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that policies and guidelines on Overtime Services and Overtime Pay for Government Employees as prescribed under existing laws, rules and regulations and currently amended by CSC and DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015 should be strictly followed in view of its efficient and effective implementation and the national government’s directive to adopt austerity measures in the operations of the government, as a whole. 

Require the refund of the overtime pay claimed by the respective Department Heads and refrain from claiming such in future transactions to avoid possible disallowance in audit.

It is also required that overtime pay claims should be duly processed; that is, duly authorized and properly documented, before it will be paid to avoid possible suspension in audit.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, the LGU had already stopped the practice of payment of overtime services to department heads. Further, payment of overtime services to its employees were now based and in accordance with CSC and DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe the proper and faithful adherence to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations in all of the LGU’s procurement activities so as not to defeat the purpose of the law to have a judicious use of the scarce government resources, so as to ascertain the legality, validity, and propriety of transactions and prevent government funds from misuse, loss or wastage.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management faithfully adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9003, known as the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” especially on the creation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board that is tasked to prepare and approve a long-term SWMP that will set guidelines and targets for solid waste reduction and disposal to avoid using of funds entirely for wages of job order personnel.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, the Management still failed to submit the MSWMP to our end to validate the disbursements under this fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management faithfully comply with the requirements of Section 272 of RA 7160 in the application of proceeds of the additional One Percent SEF Tax so as to serve the purpose for which the law was created and give benefits to the LGU’s constituents particularly the needs of the education sector.
observations.reason: The Management admitted their failure for the past years but for CY 2016 transactions the Municipal Budget Officer acted and complied strictly the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the requirements of the budgetary limitation as provided for under Section 325 (a) of RA 7160 to ensure that the LGU shall have a balance budget and that funds are allocated to the delivery of basic services and development projects.
